title: Pruthvi Jana vs Hoovappa S/O Erappa on 5 July, 2023
body: Pruthvi Jana vs Hoovappa S/O Erappa on 5 July, 2023 Author: S.R.Krishna Kumar Bench: S.R.Krishna Kumar -1- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU DATED THIS THE 5TH DAY OF JULY, 2023 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR JUSTICE S.R.KRISHNA KUMAR WRIT PETITION NO.11994 OF 2023 (GM- CPC ) BETWEEN: 1. PRUTHVI JANA D/O B.J.SATEESH, C/O GOWRAMMA, W/O B.J.SREEPADRAO, AGED ABOUT 24 YEARS, 2. POORNA JANA D/O B.J.SATEESH, C/O GOWRAMMA, W/O B.J.SREEPADRAO, AGED ABOUT 22 YEARS, PETITIONERS ARE RESIDENTS OF KEREKOPPA VILLAGE, ULAVI HOBLI, SORABA TALUK-577429, Digitally SHIVAMOGGA DISTRICT. signed by ...PETITIONERS VANDANA S (BY SRI. S V PRAKASH, ADVOCATE) Location: High Court of AND: Karnataka 1. HOOVAPPA S/O ERAPPA, AGED ABOUT 53 YEARS, R/O KODAKANI VILLAGE, KASABA HOBLI, SORABA TALUK-577 429. SHIVAMOGGA DISTRICT. 2. SHOBHA W/O SATEESH B.J., AGED ABOUT 45 YEARS, -2- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 3. SATEESH B J S/O LATE SREEPAD RAO, AGED ABOUT 51 YEARS, RESPONDENTS NO.2 AND 3 ARE RESIDENT OF KEREKOPPA VILLAGE, ULAVI HOBLI, SORABA TALUK-577 429, SHIVAMOGGA DISTRICT. ...RESPONDENTS THIS WP IS FILED UNDER ARTICLE 227 OF THE CONSTITUTION OF INDIA PRAYING TO QUASH THE ORDER DTD 30.03.2023 PASSED BY THE COURT OF LEARNED SENIOR CIVIL JUDGE AND JMFC SORABA TO THE EXTENT REJECTING THE APPLICATION UNDER IA NO.II IN EX. PETITION NO.29/2019 PRODUCED AS PER ANNX-J TO THE WP. THIS PETITION, COMING ON FOR PRELIMINARY HEARING, THIS DAY, THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING: ORDER This petition by the impleading applicants in Ex.No.29/2019 on the file of the Senior Civil Judge and JMFC, Soraba (for short "Trial Court") is directed against the impugned order dated 30.03.2023, whereby the application, I.A.No.2 filed by the petitioners-impleading applicants seeking impleadment in the aforesaid execution proceedings as additional judgment debtor Nos.3 and 4 was dismissed by the Trial Court. 2. Heard learned counsel for the petitioners and perused the material on record. -3- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 3. The material on record discloses that respondent No.1- decree holder has instituted the aforesaid execution proceedings against respondent Nos.2 and 3-judgment debtors to enforce, implement and execute the judgment and decree for specific performance and other reliefs granted in his favour against respondent Nos.2 to 3 in O.S.No.50/2014 dated 02.11.2018. During the pendency of the said execution proceedings, the petitioners herein claiming to be children of the judgment debtors filed the instant application seeking impleadment and the said application having been opposed by the decree holder, the Trial Court proceeded to pass the impugned order rejecting I.A.No.2 by holding as under: "ORDER ON I.A. NO. II AND III The applicants had filed I.A. No.II under Order I Rule 10 r/w. Section of C.P.C to come on record, as J.Dr. No.3 and 4, for the reasons stated in the affidavit enclosed with the application. 2. In the affidavit, the applicants have deposed that the decree holder has filed this petition for execution of decree in O.S. No. 50/2014 dated 02.11.2018 and to get execute the sale deed from Judgment debtor, as per the decree. Further, the applicants have stated that the judgment debtors are their parents. They have stated that 1 acre 20 -4- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 gunte in Sy. No. 97/2, 1 acre 22 gunte in Sy. No. 126 and 36 gunte in Sy. No. 127 and 2 acre 18 gunte in sy. No.98 are their joint family properties. Further, they stated that in O. S.153 /2007, this court has passed decree on 19.04.2008. According to that decree, the aforesaid properties have came to their family. Further, they stated that 1 acre 20 gunte land in Sy. No.97/2 of Kerekoppa village is the property acquired from the joint family income and purchased in the name of judgment debtor herein. They stated that his property is also the joint family properties of them and the judgment debtors. Further, they stated that they filed the suit for their separate possession in the property involved in this case. They stated that the decree in O. S. 50 /2014 is not binding upon them. Further, the applicants have stated that in view of decree in O. S.44 /2020, they have got 1/3rd share in Sy.No. 97/2P, measuring 1 acre 20 gunte of Kerekoppa village. Therefore, the applicants have contended that they are necessary parties in this execution proceedings. 3. The applicants have filed I.A.No. III under Order VIII Rule 1-A (3) of C.P.C with a list of documents and requested to grant permission to produce those documents. In the affidavit enclosed with this application, the applicants have stated that the documents sought to be produced are very much necessary to appreciate the case. Therefore, it is requested to allow the application. 4. The counsel for the decree holder has filed objections to both applications and contended that the facts mentioned in the affidavit are false and the applicants are not -5- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 necessary parties to the proceedings. Further, stated that this petition is filed for execution of decree in O. S.50 /2014 dated 02.11.2014. Further, it is stated that in the sale agreement dated 14.05.2013, the judgment debtor No.1 and 2 herein, executed agreement on their behalf and also on behalf of the applicants herein, as their guardians. Therefore, the learned counsel has submitted that there is no merit in the application. Hence, it is requested to dismiss the application. 5. Having heard the submission of both side counsel, I have carefully perused the applications and objections on record. 6. The points for my consideration are: 1. Whether the applicants are necessary parties to this proceedings? 2. Whether the applicant No.1 and 2 made out a case to produce the documents? 3. What Order? 7. My answer to the above points as under: Point No.1: In the Negative Point No.2: In the Affirmative Point No.3: As per final order the following: REASONS 8. Point No. 1 and 2: This petition has been filed for execution of decree in O. S. 50 /2014. The decree holder -6- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 has got specific performance of decree in O. S. 50 /2014, wherein, the defendants, who are the judgment debtors herein, are hereby directed to execute the regular sale deed in favour of the plaintiff, who is the decree older herein, in terms of the agreement of sale deed dated 14.05.2013, by receiving balance consideration of Rs.2,00,000/- within one month from the date of order, failing which, the plaintiff is at liberty to get registered the regular sale deed from the court. In order to execute this decree, the decree holder has deposited balance amount of Rs.2,00,000/- with the permission of the court and when the matter is posted for further step to get execute the sale deed through the Court Commissioner, the applicants have filed these two applications. 9. The applicants have claimed themselves as sons of J.Drs. They contended that the suit schedule property in O. S. 50 /2014 is joint family property and they got share by filing separate suit in O. S.44 /2020. Therefore, it is the contention of the applicants that the decree in O. S.50 /2014 cannot be executed, without their presence. Along with I.A No. II, the applicants have produced copy of judgment and decree in O. S. 44 /2020. On perusal of the documents, it reveals that these applicants have got share in Sy.No.97/2 of Kerekoppa village, measuring 1 acre 20 gunte, which is one of the suit schedule property in O.S. No. 50/2014. On perusal of the judgment in O. S. 44 /2020, filed by the applicants against the judgment debtors herein, there is no reference regard to the judgment and decree in O. S. 50 /2014, which has been decreed on 02.11.2018, much prior to the decree in -7- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 O. S. 44 /2020. Further, it reveals that the applicants herein, are claiming their rights over the property involved in this execution proceedings through judgment debtors. In my considered view, the applicants are not having any independent rights over the suit schedule property in O. S.50 /2014. Further, the presence of these applicants is not at all necessary for execution of decree in O. S. 50 /2014. The applicants have filed I.A. No. II to produce the documents. In view of aforesaid discussion, I have answered point No.1 in the Negative and point No.2 in the Affirmative. 10. Point No. 3: In view of my answers to Point No. 1 and 2, I proceed to pass following: ORDER I.A. No. III filed under Order VIII Rule 1-A (3) of C.P.C by the applicants is hereby allowed. Further, I.A No. II filed under Order 1 Rue 10 of C.P.C ., by the applicants is hereby dismissed with cost of Rs. 300/-". 4. Upon re-appreciation, re-evaluation and reconsideration of the material on record along with the impugned order, I am of the view that the impugned order does not suffer from any illegality or infirmity nor can the same is said to have occasioned failure of justice warranting interference by this Court in exercise of its jurisdiction under Article 227 of the Constitution of India as held by the Apex Court in the case of Radhey Shyam Vs. -8- NC: 2023:KHC:23460 WP No. 11994 of 2023 Chhabi Nath reported in (2015) 5 SCC 423. However, it is made clear that the judgment and decree passed in O.S.No.50/2014, as well as any compromise, settlement, judgment, order, decree etc., passed/to be passed in Ex.No.29/2019 will not be binding upon the petitioners nor affect or cause prejudice to their alleged right, title, interest, possession etc., if any, in the suit schedule property. All rival contentions between the petitioners and respondents are kept open and no opinion is expressed on the same. 5. Subject to the aforesaid directions and observations, the writ petition is disposed of without interfering with the impugned order. Sd/- JUDGE BMC
